How Serpentine Rivers Form

By Jane Doe on July 1, 2026

Rivers rarely run straight. Over time a channel begins to meander, carving broad loops across a floodplain. The physics is covered in depth by the USGS water science team.

The outside of each bend erodes while the inside accretes sediment, so the whole pattern migrates downstream. This is the essence of a meandering system.

The mechanism

Three forces interact to shape the channel:

  • Centrifugal flow driving water to the outer bank
  • Helical secondary currents moving bedload inward
  • Bank cohesion resisting erosion

Stages of development

  1. An initial gentle bend
  2. Amplification into a full loop
  3. Neck cutoff and an oxbow lake
A river is the report card for its watershed.

Read more in our rivers series, or explore the raw flow data.
